response_raw:
## 1. Rider objective & context

The sequence shows what appears to be a group ride on an off-road enduro/trail route through forested, hilly terrain. At least two riders are visible in early frames. The clip transitions from a stationary pause to an attempted uphill climb on a narrow dirt track, ending with the motorcycle on the ground.

---

## 2. Rider

- Wearing a white/black Leatt helmet, black jersey/pants, neon yellow/green 100% gloves, and red motocross boots
- Carrying a blue hydration backpack
- In frames 1–3: seated or standing astride the motorcycle, stationary, looking ahead/down
- In frames 7–8: camera perspective shifts to first-person (helmet-mounted or chest-mounted), showing the rider's gloved hands on the handlebars
- In later frames: both gloved hands are visible gripping the bars; one boot is visible on the ground, indicating the motorcycle is no longer upright and moving

---

## 3. Terrain & environment

- Autumn/early spring deciduous and coniferous mixed forest; trees are bare or lightly leafed
- Overcast sky, diffuse light
- Dirt track with visible ruts, loose rocks, gravel patches, scattered fallen leaves, and moss
- Steep uphill gradient visible in frames 4–7; the track narrows significantly going uphill into the tree line
- Surrounding vegetation includes bare shrubs, small evergreen bushes, and pine/fir trees
- The hillside has loose, damp-looking soil

---

## 4. Events

- **Frames 1–3:** A rider on a red GASGAS motorcycle is stationary on a wide dirt area near dense scrub. A second motorcycle (also red) is partially visible in the foreground, suggesting a group pause. The rider looks forward and down at various points.
- **Frames 4–6:** The camera (now forward-facing, likely helmet or chest mount) shows the track ahead: a wide rutted dirt track transitioning into a narrow, steep uphill single-track leading into the forest.
- **Frame 7:** The camera is moving up the steep narrow track; the red front fender of the motorcycle is visible at the bottom of the frame.
- **Frame 8:** The camera angle tilts sharply to the right and downward — the view shows treetops and then the ground lateral to the bike, consistent with the motorcycle tipping or falling to the right side.
- **Frames 9–15:** The camera is now looking down at the motorcycle from above and to the side. The bike is lying on its right side on the ground. The handlebars are twisted. Both gloved hands are visible interacting with the bars/controls. A booted foot is visible on the ground beside the downed bike. The bike remains stationary on the hillside dirt surface.

---

## 5. Outcome

The motorcycle is on its right side on the ground on or near the steep uphill section. The rider's hands and one foot are visible alongside the downed machine in the final frames. The bike has not been righted within the visible frames.

---

## 6. Unclear or not visible

- The exact moment of the fall is not captured between frames — it occurs in the transition between frame 8 and frame 9
- Whether the rider sustained any injury is not visible
- The second rider's status after frame 3 is not visible
- Engine state (running or stalled) is not determinable from still frames
- The exact cause of the fall (e.g., wheel slip, obstacle contact) is not visible
- The rider's facial expression and full body posture in later frames are not visible (camera is helmet/chest mounted)
- Speed at time of fall is not determinable
